message.content:
(1) The phone mockup preview frame occupies approximately 55-60% of the screen width, centered and taking up the majority of the left side. It’s a vertical rectangular frame with rounded corners, containing the video preview.  

(2) The timer/counter ring inside the preview is **oversized**—it spans roughly 30-35% of the preview’s height, with a thin circular border and a large, bold “3” (current count) in the center, plus “/1000” (total) below. It’s positioned in the upper-middle of the preview, overlaying the sunset background.  

(3) Yes, a play button is visible. It’s a **circular white button with a purple play icon**, located directly in the center of the preview (overlapping the timer ring). It’s prominent and sized to be easily tappable.  

(4) The overall layout is **usable but preview-dominant**: The preview takes up ~55-60% of the width, while the right sidebar (controls) occupies ~40-45%. The preview is left-aligned/centered, with the sidebar to its right. The preview dominates visually but doesn’t overwhelm the interface—controls (Zikr Selection, Customize, Export) are clearly separated and accessible. Proportions balance visual focus on the preview with functional space for settings.
